title: aegilops triuncialis subsp. kotschyi
intro: Aegilops triuncialis subsp. kotschyi, also known as goat grass or wild emmer, is a subspecies of the wild emmer wheat plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Aegilops triuncialis subsp. kotschyi include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
